---

## Figure Type

This is a **geophysical cross-section (profile)**, specifically a combined gravity/magnetic or seismic interpretation profile with an accompanying geological-geophysical section panel below it. It appears to be a **Профиль VII (Profile VII)** — a interpreted subsurface geophysical profile typical of Soviet regional surveys, showing both the observed geophysical curve (upper portion) and the interpreted geological cross-section (lower panel with formation labels and physical property values).

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers **Profile VII (Профиль VII)** as indicated in the title at the top center. Based on the report context (Report 36572, Smolianova et al., 1983–1984), this profile is located within the northern Kazakhstan survey area covering map sheets **M-42 and N-42** at 1:200,000 scale. The profile appears to extend horizontally across several hundred kilometers (or kilometers at local scale), with horizontal distance markings visible along the baseline (values appearing to read approximately 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800 units — likely in km or profile-km). The lower geological panel references geological units including what appear to be **PR (Proterozoic)** formations and possibly Paleozoic units, with borehole or point designations numbered **1 through 5** along the profile.

---

## Key Content

**Upper Panel (Geophysical Curve):**
- A prominent, strongly asymmetric **anomaly curve** dominates the upper portion of the figure. The curve is relatively flat on the left side near baseline, then rises steeply to a very high peak (reaching approximately **+20 to +26 units** on the vertical scale — likely milligals for gravity or nanoteslas/gammas for magnetics) near the center-left of the profile, then descends steeply and remains elevated before another moderate peak to the right.
- The vertical axis on the left shows values in what appear to be **milligals (мГл)** or similar geophysical units, with gridlines at regular intervals. Values range from approximately **-2 to +26** units, with a zero baseline marked by a horizontal line across the middle of the upper panel.
- A **negative anomaly region** (below baseline) is visible at the left portion of the profile, suggesting a density or susceptibility low.
- Two or three **subsidiary peaks** are visible on the right half of the profile, suggesting multiple subsurface source bodies or structural highs.
- Points labeled **①, ②, ③** and possibly more are marked along the curve at key inflection or peak positions.

**Lower Panel (Geological Interpretation Section):**
- This panel shows a **layered geological cross-section** correlated to the geophysical curve above.
- Horizontal layers are drawn representing different geological formations, bounded by interpreted faults or contacts shown as vertical or oblique lines.
- Numbered blocks **(1 through 5)** subdivide the section horizontally, likely corresponding to structural blocks or survey segments.
- **Physical property values** (density in г/см³ or susceptibility values) are annotated within each block/layer, including values such as:
  - **+0.05 г/см³**, **+0.03 г/см³**, **+0.047 г/см³**, **+0.10 г/см³**, **+0.18 г/см³** (or similar — these are likely density contrast values Δσ)
  - **-0.03 г/см³** or similar negative contrasts indicating lower-density bodies
  - Values in the format **×10⁻³ г/см³** or **×10⁻⁴ СГС** for magnetic susceptibility
- Formation labels visible include **γQ** (Quaternary granites or sediments?), **PR₂** (Upper Proterozoic), **PR₁** (Lower Proterozoic), and possibly **γ** symbols indicating **granitoid intrusions**.
- Depth scale on the left of the lower panel shows values from **0 to approximately -8 to -10 km**, indicating the section reaches to considerable crustal depth.
- Vertical lines within the section suggest **fault boundaries** separating blocks of different physical properties.

**Title:** Профиль VII (Profile VII)

**Top left corner:** 1964 г. (year 1964, possibly a base map date)

- **PR₁γ** — Lower Proterozoic granitoids
